But you have to understand, [[Spider-Woman Suit|this mask]] is my badge, and I'm trying to be good too. I was trying so hard to wear this thing the way you would want, and I didn't... I didn't. I can do all these things, but I can't help the people I love the most, and they can only know half of who I am, so I-I'm completely on my own, and now I don't even know what the right thing is anymore! I don't know what I'm supposed to do, but I know... I can't lose one more friend.|[[Gwendolyn Stacy (Earth-65)|Spider-Woman]]|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse}} '''Gwendolyn Maxine "Gwen" Stacy''' is a [[Visions Academy]] student who was bitten by a [[radioactive spider]] which granted her superhuman abilities causing her to become the super-heroine known as '''Spider-Woman'''. She would go on to become her city's line of defense against multiple villains including [[Otto Octavius (Earth-65)|Doctor Octopus]] and the [[Adrian Toomes (Earth-65)|Vulture]]. Unfortunately, after failing to save her best friend [[Peter Parker (Earth-65)|Peter Parker]], her guilt for his death caused her to attempt to distance and isolate herself from others. A couple of years later after these events, Gwen was pulled out of her dimension by [[Wilson Fisk (Earth-1610)|Kingpin's]] [[Super-Collider]] into [[Earth-1610|another universe]] where she would meet and befriend [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]] and alternate versions of her deceased best friend. After Miles showed he was ready to be Spider-Man, she along the rest of the [[Spider-Gang]] managed to stop Kingpin before he caused irreparable damage to the [[Multiverse]]. After this, Gwen returned to her home universe and continued with her life as Spider-Woman. After a fight with an alternate version of the [[Adriano Tumino|Vulture]] and her father attempting to arrest her, Gwen was recruited by [[Miguel O'Hara (Earth-928)|Miguel O'Hara]] and [[Jessica Drew (Earth-332)|Jessica Drew]] into the [[Spider-Society]], managing to be mentored by the latter. As she began to protect the Multiverse from any threat, she would befriend [[Pavitr Prabhakar]] and formed a romantic bond with [[Hobart Brown (Earth-138)|Hobie Brown]], going on various missions with them. After The [[Jonathan Ohnn|Spot]] started to gain the abilities to travel between the [[:Category:Dimensions|alternate dimensions]], Gwen Stacy was tasked by Jessica Drew and Miguel O'Hara to go to Earth-1610 and track the villain. However, Gwen could not pass up on an opportunity to finally see Miles for the first time in over a year, resulting in Spot gaining more power. After Miles followed her to [[Earth-50101]], he would discover that his [[Jefferson Morales (Earth-1610)|father]] is going to die. Begrudgingly, Gwen would participate in the chase to capture Miles alongside the Spider-Society. After Miles escaped from the society, she would be sent back to her home universe by Miguel O'Hara. With nowhere to go, she would fix her relationship with her [[George Stacy (Earth-65)|dad]], and she would get [[Web-Watch|a bootleg version of the Multiversal Gizmo]] created by Hobie. After realizing Miles was in a [[Earth-42|different universe]] and promising his parents that she'd bring their son home, she would use the gizmo to recruit all her friends to form a new incarnation of the [[Spider-Gang]] in an attempt to rescue her friend. ==Biography== {{Quote|All right, people! Let's start at the beginning one last time. My name is Gwen Stacy. I was bitten by a radioactive spider. And for the last two years, I've been the one and only Spider-Woman! You guys know the rest. I joined a band. Saved my dad. I couldn't save my best friend, Peter Parker... so now I save everyone else. And I don't do friends anymore... just to avoid any distractions. And one day this weird thing happened... and I mean, like, REALLY weird. I was blown into last week. LITERALLY! I landed in New York, but... not MY New York. My spider-sense told me to head to Visions Academy. Wasn't sure why until I met you.|Gwen Stacy introducing herself in [[Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse|Into the Spider-Verse]]|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}} ===Early Life=== ====Life in Chelsea==== [[File:Gwen Stacy says grace with Peter, May, Ben and her father George.jpg|thumb|left|250x250px|Gwen says grace with her father and the Parker family]] Gwendolyn Maxine "Gwen" Stacy was born in the 2000s in the Chelsea borough of New York City. She was born as the only daughter of police officer [[George Stacy (Earth-65)|George Stacy]] and his wife [[Helen Stacy (Earth-65)|Helen Stacy]]. Helen died early on in Gwen's childhood, resulting in George having to raise her on his own and Gwen not getting to know her mother well, if at all. George's career as a police officer would inspire Gwen to live to uphold the law and help others. George was close friends with the Parker family who were their neighbour's. Gwen met and befriended [[Peter Parker (Earth-65)|Peter Parker]] in her childhood, forming a close bond with him throughout the years and grew to consider him her only friend. ====Becoming Spider-Woman==== {{Biography Quote|Touch him again, Ned!|Gwen Stacy to [[Ned Leeds (Earth-65)]]|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse}}[[File:Touchhimagainned.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Gwen defends [[Peter Parker (Earth-65)|Peter]] from [[Ned Leeds (Earth-65)|Ned]]]] In her teenage years, Gwen was enrolled by her father at [[Visions Academy]] alongside Peter. At Visions, she met and befriended [[Mary Jane Watson (Earth-65)|Mary Jane]], [[Betty Brant]] and [[Glory Grant]], and joined their punk rock band "[[Mary Janes|The Mary Janes]]" as their drummer. Gwen became interested and in ballet, and also joined the school gymnastics team. Her father supported her in these activities. Unfortunately, Peter did not have the same luck as Gwen and became isolated due to his "nerdy" appearance and interests. This caused Peter to become an easy target for bullying from other students, especially [[Ned Leeds (Earth-65)|Ned Leeds]]. Gwen would often stand up for Peter but instead of helping the bullying to stop, it only caused it to increase as it made Peter look weak in front of others, unknowingly furthering his humiliation and fuelling his sense of worthlessness and despair. [[File:The Spider (Gwen E-65) 001.png|thumb|250x250px|Gwen is bitten by the radioactive spider|left]] In late [[2010s/Earth-65|2016]], a twelve/thirteen year old[https://www.twinkl.com/teaching-wiki/spider-gwen#:~:text=Gwen%20Stacy%20is%20a%20high,responsibility%20for%20someone%20so%20young! https://www.twinkl.com/teaching-wiki/spider-gwen#:~:text=Gwen%20Stacy%20is%20a%20high,responsibility%20for%20someone%20so%20young!] Gwen was bitten by a [[Radioactive Spider|radioactive spider]], giving her superhuman powers and abilities. These included superhuman strength and reflexes, as well as an ability to adhere to walls, and a precognitive sense that could prevent her from being hurt by any danger. Being inspired by her father's career, she resolved to use her newfound spider-like abilities to fight crime and help others as she became the vigilante known as Spider-Woman. ====Life as Spider-Woman==== {{Biography Quote|You're in this to help people, right? Well so am I!|Gwen Stacy to [[George Stacy (Earth-65)|George Stacy]]|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse}}[[File:GwenasSpider-Woman.png|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Woman as a vigilante]] As Spider-Woman, Gwen started her crime fighting career by attempting to defend New York from any threat. Due to her acts of vigilantism, she often got into disputes with her father who had no idea that the vigilante was his own daughter. She ended up saving him once from a potentially fatal gunshot wound but this would not improve her father's view on Spider-Woman at all. As Spider-Woman, Gwen would deepen her voice whenever she spoke to hide her identity. At some point during her crime fighting career, Gwen would be buried alive before managing to escape the grave. [[File:Gwenpeter.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Gwen talking with Peter|left]] Although Spider-Woman wasn't particularly liked by her father, Peter gained a fanaticism for her. Often, Peter would talk to Gwen about the vigilante and show her pictures that he had taken of Spider-Woman. During these conversations, Peter revealed to her that he thinks that Spider-Woman is pretty, much to Gwen's amusement. Her father would often interrupt these conversations as he gave his own more critical thoughts about Spider-Woman.''[[Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse]]''''[[Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse]]'' ====Losing Peter==== {{Biography Quote|I just... wanted to be special, like you... Gwen.|[[Peter Parker (Earth-65)|Peter Parker]] to Spider-Woman|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse}} [[File:GHApKhHXYAAlNv1.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Gwen at the Homecoming Dance]] At a Homecoming Dance, Gwen had planned to come as Peter's prom date. Upon arriving at the dance, Gwen tried to find Peter but could not find him anywhere. Before she could think much further, her Spider-Sense went off and a giant mutant creature known as the Lizard appeared and immediately attacked the party goers. [[File:Fightingthelizard.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Woman fights the monstrous [[Peter Parker (Earth-65)|Lizard]]|left]] Quickly changing into her [[Spider-Woman Suit|Spider-Woman suit]], Gwen rushed into battle against the creature as it attempted to attack Ned Leeds. The Lizard slammed Gwen into a wall before she carelessly kicked the beast into a pillar causing the entire ceiling of the building to collapse onto him. Seeing that the Lizard had been defeated, Gwen rushed to the creature's body only to find that it was her best friend Peter. Grabbing Peter, she tried to understand what had happened but Peter simply told her that he just wanted to be special like her. Upon Peter using her real name, Gwen grabbed his hand in shock over the revelation that he knew her real identity all along, preventing him from seeing her tearful face as she begged him not to go. Peter died in her arms as she held him close and sobbed into his body. [[File:Rippeter.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Woman mourns the loss of Peter]] Soon after, officer Stacy arrived on the scene to investigate what had happened only to see Spider-Woman holding a dead body. In fear of being confronted by her father, a tearful Gwen swung away. George realized the body that Spider-Woman had been crouched over was none other than that of his daughter's best friend. Coming to the deduction that Spider-Woman had murdered Peter and escaped from the scene of the crime, George began a massive manhunt to find and apprehend the vigilante. ==== Acting Solo==== {{Biography Quote|I never really made another friend after that.|Gwen Stacy|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse}}[[File:sadgwen.png|thumb|250x250px|Gwen isolates herself from others|left]] After these events Gwen distanced herself from others as a way to avoid "distractions" as well as to avoid losing someone else, assuming that anyone close to her would get hurt just like Peter. She began to take her Spider-Woman career much more seriously as she attempted to avoid making the same mistake she had made before and to somehow repay the moral debt she had gained after accidentally killing Peter. She continued to be a member of the Mary Jane's in order to release her feelings of anger, guilt and loss through her drumming but would distance herself from the band members as her guilt and depression from Peter's death grew over time. Eventually, she completely avoided talking to them and only saw them when they had to rehearse for concerts. Although her alter ego was being sought by the law due to Peter Parker's death, she defended the city from many super villains who threatened to terrorize it. Among those villains were [[Adrian Toomes (Earth-65)|Vulture]], who became a recurring villain for the heroine, as well as [[Otto Octavius (Earth-65)|Doctor Octopus]] and Kraven the Hunter. She also got into brief feuds with her father as both Gwen Stacy and Spider-Woman due to his attempts to catch her at all costs. ===[[Collider Crisis]]=== ====Arriving to another universe==== {{Biography Quote|And one day, this weird thing happened. And I mean, like, really weird. I was blown into last week, literally. I landed in New York, but not my New York.|Spider-Woman|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}}[[File:Gwen arriving 1610.jpeg|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Woman is transported to [[Earth-1610]]]] Around two years after she became Spider-Woman, Gwen was fighting against Doctor Octopus. However, in the middle of her fight she was pulled into a portal. The portal pulled her into another [[Earth-1610|dimension]], landing her a week before the portal opened up in that world. In front of a screen, she saw the news of the [[Peter Parker (Earth-1610)|Spider-Man]] of that world and realized she was no longer in her New York. ====Meeting Miles Morales==== {{Biography Quote|I'm Gwe-aaaanda?"
"You're name is Gwanda?"
"It's African...I'm South African|Gwen Stacy and [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]]|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}}[[File:GwenMeetsMiles.png|thumb|250x250px|Gwen meets [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]]|left]] Not too long after entering this new universe, her spider-sense told her to head to [[Brooklyn Visions Academy|Visions Academy]]. Gwen infiltrated it, managing to pose as just another student at the school. Gwen would first meet [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles]] in physics class after Miles had told a bad joke about Einstein and sat next to her due to her being the only person who had laughed at it. Initially thinking he was cute, Gwen would attempt to strike conversation but was interrupted by the teacher. [[File:GwenAwkward.jpg|thumb|250x250px|"Gwanda" introduces herself]] The next day, Gwen intentionally bumped into Miles. She'd immediately be weirded out by how much Miles was sweating, only for Miles to claim the most unbelievable set of excuses. Miles would introduce himself by name and Gwen awkwardly introduced herself as Gwanda, not wanting to give away her real name in the event there was another version of herself in this universe. She claimed she was African before correcting herself to say she was South African and that she didn't have an accent because she had been raised in Brooklyn. [[File:TheShoulderTouch.png|thumb|250x250px|Miles attempts the shoulder touch on "Gwanda"|left]] Attempting to hit on her with his [[Aaron Davis (Earth-1610)|uncle's]] advice, Miles initiated the shoulder touch. With Gwen being unimpressed by his flirting attempt, Miles would try to pull his hand away from Gwen only for it to get stuck in the right side of her hair. Gwen knew Miles was experiencing his powers for the first time and told him to calm down and relax but he would not listen as he was too confused. Miles attempting to find a solution tried to aggressively pull his hand out of her hair only for Gwen to counter by throwing him over her head. [[File:GwenGoofyCut.png|thumb|250x250px|"Gwanda" is forced to shave half her head]] Gwen and Miles were taken to the school nurse's office not too long after where half of Gwen's head was shaved to remove Miles's hand, leaving a giant hand print, much to her dismay. Walking outside of the office, a livid Gwen would see Miles walking on a wall wearing nothing but a pair of pants while birds were attached to his hands. ====Alchemax Incident==== {{Biography Quote|You don't get to like my haircut|Gwen Stacy to [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]]|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}} [[File:GwenNerd.png|thumb|251x251px|An undercover Gwen watches [[Olivia Octavius]]|left]] Gwen would get herself a proper shave and haircut to remove the giant handprint that had been left in her hair, adopting a shaved side undercut. Gwen then began to follow Miles more earnestly and tracked him to [[Alchemax]]. Using the disguise of being a scientist at the facility, Gwen gained access to the lab and briefly bumped into Miles who was fleeing his universe's [[Olivia Octavius|Doctor Octopus]] alongside a variant of [[Peter Parker (Earth-616)|Peter Parker]]. [[File:GwenBadass.png|thumb|251x251px|Spider-Woman assists [[Peter Parker (Earth-616)|Peter B. Parker]] and [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles]]]] Finding the duo in the forest, Gwen engaged in battle with Doctor Octopus as Spider-Woman and quickly defeated her. With Octavius defeated, Gwen revealed her identity to Miles and the variant of her deceased best friend who introduced himself as [[Peter Parker (Earth-616)|Peter B. Parker]]. Miles was shocked that she was also a Spider-Person, and he proceeded to compliment her haircut, to which Gwen responded by angrily saying he was not allowed to like it, as she was still upset over it. [[File:Gwenmilesselfie.jpg|thumb|222x222px|Gwen takes a selfie with [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]]|left]] The trio would get on an approximately 4 hour long bus ride as they made their way back to the city. With Peter passed out, Gwen and Miles would get to know each other better. Gwen would talk with Miles about losing her best friend. Miles would offer to be Gwen's friend if she was ever interested in trying that out again, Gwen responding by telling him that she'd keep him posted. At some point during this bus ride, Gwen and Miles would take a selfie together. ====Meeting other Spider-People==== [[File:Spider-Sense (Spider-Gang) 001.png|thumb|251x251px|Spider-Woman meets more [[Peter Parker (Earth-90214)|var]][[Peni Parker|ian]][[Peter Porker|ts]] of herself]] Upon arriving back in the city, the trio made their way to this world's [[May Parker (Earth-1610)|Aunt May]]. After a short talk with Peter, May takes the trio to the shed in her backyard where this world's Peter had kept all his suit designs. Here they meet three other [[Spider-People]], [[Peter Parker (Earth-90214)|Spider-Man Noir]], [[Peni Parker]], and [[Peter Porker|Spider-Ham]], who had all been sent to this universe by the collider as well. Here, they all discussed their plan to use the [[Super-Collider]] to return them to their homes. Peter informed them that one of them would have to stay behind to destroy the machine, risking disintegration due to being in a different dimension, for which Gwen and every other Spider-Person volunteered. Miles then told them that he would be the one to destroy the machine so they could all go home. All the others, excluding Peter, questioned Miles and pressured him to the point that he fled the shed, defeated after being pushed too hard. ====[[Duel at Aunt May's House]]==== [[File:Spider-Gang 001.png|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Woman prepares to fight|left]] A hurt Miles left to figure things out on his own, only to return once the others created the new [[goober]] for the machine, with news that [[Aaron Davis (Earth-1610)|his uncle was the Prowler]] and one of [[Wilson Fisk (Earth-1610)|Kingpin's]] enforcers. The group asked Miles if he had been followed, as all Kingpin's enforcers, including the Prowler, burst through the door, ambushing the group in May's house. During the battle, Gwen focused on the [[Maximus Gargan (Earth-1610)|Scorpion]] and she managed to lure him away from the house.[[File:Spider-Gang 002.png|thumb|250x250px|Spider-Woman and the [[Spider-Gang|gang]] prepare to hide from [[Ganke Lee (Earth-1610)|Ganke Lee]]]]Some time after the battle had died down, the group tracked Miles to his dorm room and tried to console him for the death of his uncle. All the spider people spoke of how they had gone through something similar including Gwen who brought up the death of Peter. Out of nowhere, Miles's roommate Ganke entered the room and the group hid on the ceiling. Unfortunately for them, Ganke spotted them and fainted right after Spider-Ham spoke. Peter then confirmed that the decision had been made that he would be the one to stay behind. Tying Miles up, the group left. Gwen bid him goodbye and waited outside with the others, hoping that Miles would be able to prove to Peter that he was ready, to no avail. ====[[Fight at the Collider]]==== [[File:GwenTeamUp.png|thumb|251x251px|Spider-Woman, [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Spider-Man]] and [[Peter Parker (Earth-616)|Spider-Man]] team up|left]] The group made their way to [[Fisk Tower]] and snuck in. Gwen called Kingpin a pig much to the dismay of Spider-Ham, Noir noticing how the waiters were dressed came up with an idea to pretend to be waiters in order to sneak into the mansion. Peter bumped into this world's Mary Jane and Gwen had to tell him to focus on the mission and avoid being distracted. They managed to arrive at the [[Super-Collider]] through Fisk's private elevator. Once there, they were almost immediately detected by Fisk's lackeys. Gwen fought them off with the others and watched Miles save Peter from [[Olivia Octavius|Doc Ock]]. In the ensuing battle, Gwen was briefly knocked out by Doc Ock and almost fell into the collider's unstable portal but was saved by Miles. Once Doc Ock and Fisk's other enforcers were no longer threats, Miles took the goober from Peter and took control of the beam, allowing the others to go home. ====Returning to Her Universe==== {{Biography Quote|Friends?
"Friends.| [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]] and Gwen Stacy|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}}[[File:GwenWantsKissMiles.png|thumb|250x250px|Gwen enters the portal]] Before she left, Miles asked Gwen if he was allowed to like her haircut now. Gwen, slyly shut down his attempt at flirting by teasing Miles that she was 15 months older, which Miles countered with an Einstein joke (about their age difference being relative) that he knew Gwen would remember when they first met. When he asked if they were friends now, Gwen agreed. Gwen jumped through the portal and returned home.'''' Two days later, Gwen looked at her selfie with Miles from the bus ride on her phone as she missed her friend and thought of their brief time together.[[File:Happy gwen.jpg|thumb|251x251px|Gwen back in her universe|left]] After returning to her universe, Gwen continued her adventures as Spider-Woman, fighting various enemies while still being part of the Mary Janes. However, her isolation that had begun from Peter's death only got worse now as she also missed Miles. Gwen ended up keeping the haircut Miles inadvertently caused her to get to remember him by and would print out a Polaroid of their selfie, placing it in her drum-set next to her Spider-Woman suit and a birthday gift from her deceased mother.'''' {{Quote|Let's do things differently this time.. like, SO differently. His name is Miles Morales. He was bitten by a radioactive spider... and he's not the only one. He hasn't always had it easy... and he's not the only one. Now he's on his own... and he's not the only one. You think you know the rest... you don't. And I thought I knew the rest, but... I didn't. I didn't wanna hurt him... but I did. And he's not the only one...|Gwen Stacy at the opening montage for [[Spider-Man:_Across_the_Spider-Verse|Spider-Man: Across the Spider Verse]]}} ===Joining the Spider-Society=== ====Quitting her band==== {{Biography Quote|I didn't join a band so I could talk about my feelings, I joined it so I could hit my feelings with sticks!|Gwen Stacy to the rest of the [[Mary Janes]]|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse}} [[File:Gwenleavesband.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Gwen quits the [[Mary Janes]]]] Around a year later, Gwen and the rest of the Mary Janes were practicing for a concert. Gwen was deep in her feelings, letting loose on her drum set, and ignored the voices of her fellow bandmates as they tried to get her to listen to them. The band members confronted Gwen and attempted to get her to open up to them about what was bothering her, only for Gwen to claim that she was fine and that she only was in band to let her feelings out through drumming. Feeling extremely isolated and misunderstood, Gwen on the verge of tears angrily kicked her drum set away and quit the band. Heading home on the subway, she thought about Miles and then her old times with Peter when he was still alive.'''' [[File:gwenhugsdad.jpg|thumb|251x251px|Gwen hugs her [[George Stacy (Earth-65)|dad]]|left]] Upon arriving at her apartment, Gwen was approached by her father who asked her how her band was doing, only for Gwen to say that she had quit. Gwen put her things away and immediately began folding her clothes, thinking to herself that she might not sleep at home tonight. George wanting to lift his daughter's spirits attempted to tell her about some new leads he had found in the case against Spider-Woman, only for Gwen to angrily shut him down by telling him that it would never bring Peter back. Feeling at a loss of words, George asked his daughter if she was too punk rock to give her old man a hug. Gwen immediately rushed and embraced her dad in a tight hug, the warm colors of her universe bending to showcase the love she felt for him. She thought this could be the last hug she'd be able to give him. ====[[Fight at the Guggenheim Museum]]==== {{Biography Quote|I'm sorry, who exactly are you supposed to be?"
"That's classified|Spider-Woman and [[Miguel O'Hara (Earth-928)|Spider-Man 2099]]|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse}} [[File:Gwengetsready.jpg|thumb|250x250px|Gwen prepares to suit up]] George's police radio went off as a disturbance was reported at the Guggenheim Museum. Once her father left, Gwen quickly turned up the radio to get information about the whereabouts of the incident. She kicked open her drum set and suited up, glancing at the Polaroid of her and Miles before closing it. Gwen swung out of her window as she made her way to the museum. [[File:Gwenmeetsmiguel.jpg|thumb|252x252px|Spider-Woman meets another [[Miguel O'Hara (Earth-928)|Spider-Man]] variant|left]]Upon arriving at the scene, she webbed up all the police officers including her father to prevent them from getting hurt. Gwen called out to Vulture only for a completely different version of the villain to attack her. Gwen fought the [[Adriano Tumino|Vulture]] variant as she guessed how he had ended up in her universe to which he agreed. Just as the Vulture was about to land a strong blow on Gwen, he was stopped by several red beams. Out of nowhere, a portal opened with a variant of Spider-Man pushing the Vulture out of the way. Gwen swung down to find out who the mysterious man was as he introduced himself as [[Miguel O'Hara (Earth-928)|Miguel O'Hara]]. [[File:Gwenkick.jpg|thumb|251x251px|Spider-Woman fights [[Adriano Tumino]]]] The duo took on the Vulture as they attempted to stop him from wrecking more havoc on the museum. Miguel told Gwen that her interference with the collider incident had not fixed the problem but actually made it worse. Miguel reluctantly called a variant of Spider-Woman, [[Jessica Drew (Earth-332)|Jessica Drew]], to join the battle. Gwen was enamoured by her and asked Jess to adopt her, much to the confusion of both Spider-Women, before continuing the fight against the Renaissance-style Vulture. Miguel rode the Vulture high into the sky but unfortunately the Vulture had caused a helicopter to plummet towards the bottom of the museum. Her usual attire is usually something punk-adjacent and is always paired with her teal colored chucks, her second pair being borrowed from Hobie. She often wears other pieces of clothing to conceal her Spider-Woman suit leaving her pointe shoes and lower half of her suit exposed. While attempting to track him down, she briefly wore a uniform which was most likely stolen from Miles's Brooklyn Visions Academy and very briefly wore a lab coat and glasses in order to disguise herself at Alchemax. ==Personality== {{Biography Quote|You and me it's-"
"We're the same... in the important ways.|Gwen Stacy and [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]]|Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse}} [[File:Gwenhugsmiles.jpg|thumb|left]] Gwen Stacy is an intelligent but highly awkward and emotionally vulnerable teenage girl. Due to the trauma of losing her best friend and mother while very young, Gwen attempts to mask her feelings but by putting up fronts and walls to appear more confident and put together than she actually is. The first time her walls are broken down post-Peter death is during the collider incident when she befriends Miles and the other members of the first Spider-Gang. After this point, her emotional state deteriorates once more as she loses the ability to mask her awkward side. When she meets Miles again a year later, she's painfully awkward but tries to instead put up fronts in order to lie to Miles about the Spider-Society's truth. She does this out of fear of hurting one of the few close friends she's made, even if it ends up backfiring. After her monologue to her father, Gwen seems more open to the idea of lowering her shields. [[File:Gwen Stacy smiles at Miles Morales.jpg|thumb|right]]Though Gwen attempted to isolate herself from others due to her own guilt and pain, it did not take long for her to open up more to the [[Spider-Gang]] and other people as she grew to bond with them due to her ability to relate to them, especially Miles. Gwen tends to bottle up her emotions and releases them when she's playing drums leading to her being particularly angry and distant. She has a tendency to snap or lash out when angry, like when she kicks her drumset down as her bandmates try to check in on her or frantically taking her multiverse watch back from Miles when he presses the wrong button. She also appears to use hugs when she is unable to clearly express her thoughts or emotions. [[File:Gwen Stacy (Civilian).jpg|left|thumb]] She cares deeply for the people close to her in her life. She is also not afraid to protect her friends and family, standing up to Ned Leeds when he bullied [[Peter Parker (Earth-65)|Peter]], and defending Miles from Miguel, even though it resulted in her being kicked out of the [[Spider-Society]]. She would reassemble the [[Spider-Gang]] with the purpose of rescuing Miles and hopefully rebuilding their friendship. == Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== {{Biography Quote|Can you swing and flip with the grace of a trained dancer?|Gwen Stacy to [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles Morales]]|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse}} *'''Spider physiology''': Having been bitten by a radioactive spider, Gwen possesses enhanced strength, durability, agility, reflexes, spider-sense, and the ability to climb walls. **'''Superhuman strength''': Gwen possesses enhanced strength, allowing her to combat foes that would otherwise overwhelm her. She can easily lift heavy debris or hurl containers that would not be able to be lifted by any ordinary human. **'''Superhuman durability''': Gwen's body is far tougher than a regular human as she can withstand falls from extreme heights, blunt force trauma, and superhuman blows with little damage. However, even with her super-durability, she is still able to be defeated if enough force is used as evidenced by her lack of power against the Go-Home Machine. **'''Superhuman agility''': Gwen possesses an enhanced levels of agility beyond the natural physical limits. She combines this natural and enhanced agility with her training as a ballerina and gymnast. **'''Wall-crawling''': Gwen possesses the ability to cling onto any surface using just her fingertips or feet, even able to walk or sit on a building upside down. **'''Spider-Sense''': When danger is present, Gwen experiences a buzzing sensation in her head as a sort of warning system, allowing her to react accordingly. Her spider-sense offers her near complete awareness of her surroundings that, in conjunction with her reflexes, allows her to instinctively dodge or counter upcoming attacks. Her spider-sense also helps her recognize other individuals with spider-powers, making them have some sort of connection. ***[[File:Tumblr f3f8e16def9da7a0967aa6f9785ae200 926ee592 540.gif|thumb]]'''Spider-Connection''': In addition, her spider-sense seems to more advanced that of the average Spider-Person, since it guided her to [[Brooklyn Visions Academy|Visions Academy]] to find Miles even before he had been bitten. It was also able to detect Miles and sense he was in danger even though he was in [[Earth-42|a different dimension]]. ===Abilities=== *'''Skilled combatant''': Gwen's spider-sense and reflexes make her a capable melee fighter. Her movements and fighting style have the grace and precision of a ballerina. She incorporates various acrobatic moves into her fighting style as well as even some judo. *'''Trained drummer''': Gwen is a trained drummer and was a member of the Mary Janes for at least 3 years before quitting and is now a member of Hobie's band. *'''Trained ballerina:''' Gwen trained as a ballerina for an unknown period of time, due to this her fighting style is very precise and pinpoint. She often walks on her tiptoes, even while wearing her chucks. Her ballet training most likely helps with her balance which is already enhanced by her spider-abilities. *'''Trained gymnast''': Due to being a member of her school's gymnastics team, Gwen had already developed a very high degree of strength even before being bitten by a radioactive spider. After being bitten by the spider, her strength increased even more, and she incorporates much of her gymnastics training into the way she swings and fights. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== *'''[[Web-Shooters]]''': Gwen is equipped with wrist-mounted shooters she uses as Spider-Woman. The Web-Shooters fire a synthetic, silk-like material meant to imitate the power and capabilities of a spider's web. As Spider-Woman, Gwen typically uses her web shooters in order to travel, but can also use these to grab people as seen when she saves [[Miles Morales]] and [[Peter B. Parker]] from falling when they were [[Collider Crisis#Infiltrating to Alchemax|escaping Alchemax]]. **'''[[Synthetic Webbing]]''': Gwen can generate webs that are a shear-thinning liquid, virtually solid until a shearing force is applied to it, rendering it fluid. [[File:Gwensuit.png|thumb]] === Other Equipment=== *'''[[Spider-Woman Suit]]''': The Spider-Woman suit is a specialized suit used by Gwen Stacy to fight crime as Spider-Woman and keep her identity secret to prevent danger to herself and her loved ones. The suit is white and black with some pink and blue web patterns in the underarm area. It also has a hood with pink and blue web patterns on the inside. The suit is one piece and extends to her wrist, ankles and bottom of her head. The mask is white with pink eye lenses. She also wears fingerless gloves that are pink with blue web patterns and go up to the elbows. She initially wore blue pointe shoes with the suit, but now wears blue Chucks gifted to her by [[Hobart Brown (Earth-138)|Hobie]]. *'''[[Web-Watch]]''': The Web-Watch, gifted to Gwen by [[Hobart Brown (Earth-138)|Hobie]], allows Gwen to maintain stability in alternate universes and travel to other dimensions with ease. === Former Equipment=== *'''[[Multiversal Gizmo]]''': The Multiversal Gizmo, created by [[Lyla]], allows Gwen to maintain stability in alternate universes and travel to other dimensions with ease as well to communicated with other members of the [[Spider-Society]]. Former coworker turned enemy **[[Metro Spider-Man]] - Former coworker turned enemy **[[LYLA]] - Former coworker turned enemy **[[Spider-Totems|Numerous other spider people]] - Former coworker turned enemy ==Trivia== === Romantic Relationships === *Gwen's feelings for Miles are complicated. In ''Into the Spider-Verse'', her feelings for Miles were initially meant to be romantic but as production shifted on the film, the relationship would become platonic. Ideas of Gwen having a kiss with Miles were shafted to instead focus on the two becoming friends and the establishment of Gwen as her own independent character. By doing so, Gwen was viewed by her own merit instead of as a love interest. In this film, Gwen is depicted turning down Miles' romantic gestures within the film, leaving the relationship as an unrequited romance. This is shown through her being unimpressed out by his attempts at flirting with her throughout the film, including the shoulder touch and his later compliment for her haircut.[https://www.buzzfeednews.com/article/adambvary/spider-man-into-the-spider-verse-miles-morales The Revolutionary Inclusion Of "Spider-Man: Into The Spider-Verse"] **In ''Across the Spider-Verse'', Gwen still doesn't respond to Miles' romantic advances positively as she cautions Miles about how Gwen Stacy falling for Spider-Man never ends well[https://collider.com/spider-man-across-the-spider-verse-miles-gwen-relationship-marvel-comics/ 'Across the Spider-Verse’: Miles & Gwen’s Comics Relationship Is Different]. On that same sequence, writer Chris Miller stated that he didn't want it to come off as too "overtly romantic" as he believes Gwen and Miles are much more than that, labelling them "kindred spirits". Spider-Verse co-writer Phil Lord has alluded that Gwen "likes him [Miles] too" but that she ultimately "can't be with him" and "doesn't want to let him down", also showcasing a belief in Gwen rejecting the idea of a romantic connection with Miles and leaving his feelings unrequited.[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=fvRWLNlT8JY&t=980s Write On: 'Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse' Writers Chris Miller, Phil Lord and Dave Callaham] Later, Lord would also state that the two were "in love" but that they "couldn't be together".[https://www.reddit.com/r/IntoTheSpiderverse/comments/1gw3idp/in_case_anyone_still_doubts_that_miles_and_gwens/ Phil Lord comments on Miles and Gwen's relationship] Spider-Verse producer Amy Pascal stated the film was a love story between Miles and Gwen[https://comicbook.com/movies/news/spider-man-across-the-spider-verse-miles-morales-gwen-stacy-love-story/ Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse Is a Love Story Between Miles and Gwen], but stated that it was an unrequited one.[https://www.reddit.com/r/IntoTheSpiderverse/comments/1cl2e36/amy_pascal_i_think_part_of_it_is_his_miles/ Amy Pascal: "I think part of it is his relationship with Gwen and this unrequited love, which are always the best love stories. The ones that don't seem to be working out."] The film's stance on Gwen's feelings remains mixed and ambiguous. **Despite Lord's statement on Gwen and Miles' being platonic within the first film, an animator who worked on the sequence believed that Gwen found Miles cute upon first seeing him.(1) Juan MiVS on X: "2/2 And Gwen was like: 1 Finally someone is not a robot 2 I liked his joke but not him 3 I mean, it was a bad joke but he is cute 4 I mean, he looks like me, but... 5 he turned to me 6 sssssshhhhhhhh" / X *In ''Across the Spider-Verse'', Gwen is implied to possibly having a romantic relationship with Hobie that began during the time that she was living with him. The extent of this relationship is not explored but is implied nevertheless. During the film, Pav makes a comment about if Miles "knows about Hobie", implying that Gwen and Hobie's relationship is public knowledge amongst those who know them. Daniel Kaluuya, voice of Hobie Brown, has stated that he believes "something was happening", bringing up how Gwen "left a lot of stuff at his house" and that their connection seems "genuine". He goes on to state that the deepness of the relationship is only known "between Gwen and Hobie. In the film's official artbook, Gwen is described as getting "starry eyed about him".Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse: The Art of the Movie The film has been stated to be a love triangle by the filmmakers with co-director Kemp Power mentioning how Hobie had given Gwen a pair of chucks, indicating closeness and fuel for the love triangle. === Additional Trivia === *In the comics, this iteration of Gwen Stacy was a member of the Mary Janes who was bitten by a radioactive spider. She initially became a hero for the money and fame but after the death of her best friend due to her own recklessness she decided to become the selfless hero known as Spider-Woman. She would eventually change her name to Ghost-Spider. *Gwen has a "'''Protect Trans Kids'''" poster above her bedroom door indicating that she is an ally of the LGBTQ+ community. There have been various queer readings of Gwen as a character with some believing that Gwen is a trans girl herself or some believing that her Peter was a trans guy. Despite all the fan discussion, there is no definitive statement given within the film in either direction. *Gwen is 15 months older than Miles, something she has teased Miles about before. *In the first film, Gwen states that she's been Spider-Woman for 2 years. This means in the second film, Gwen has been Spider-Woman for at least 3 years and 4 months, since ''[[Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse]]'' takes place a year and 4 months after ''[[Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse]]''. *Gwen is of Irish descent on her father's side, it is unknown what her mother's ethnic background is. *Gwen has made multiple allusions to a ballet or dance training past, such as her grace and fighting style, wearing pointe shoes, questioning [[Miles Morales (Earth-1610)|Miles]]' ability to move like a dancer, her preference to often walk on her tip toes and one of her sections in ''Into the Spider-Verse'''s closing credits having an entire ballet troupe and audience composed of her clones. **She also appears to have been a gymnast as her father wears the shirt of her school's gymnastics team as well as her swinging routines often involving banned gymnastics moves. * In ''Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse'', we briefly see various canon events that spider-people have gone through and there is briefly a shot of what appears to be Gwen going through the "Kraven's Last Hunt" canon event. The shot shows Gwen in her pre-Spider-Society suit implying that she has already faced off and defeated a version of Kraven the Hunter in her home universe during this event. *Gwen initially uses the name "Gwanda" when she introduces herself to Miles, in case that he somehow knew another Gwen Stacy. Miles would end up referring to her by that nickname again whenever referencing her to his parents. As of now, both Rio and Jeff believe Gwen's name to be Gwanda. *Gwen and her family are Christian, likely Irish Catholic given George's heritage. They are shown saying grace at the dinner table in Gwen's introductory monologue in ''Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse''. *Gwen owns a penguin plush which is a nod to the character of "Spider-Guin" which is the variant of Gwen Stacy from the mainline comics version of Spider-Ham's universe. This was confirmed as a reference to the character in the official script for ''Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse''. *In ''Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse'', Gwen throws Miles to the ground while he's stuck to her hair using a tomoe nage throw, a move that is often used in judo. *Based on concept art, Gwen knows how to use a skateboard and is able to fold her clothes and type on her phone using her bare feet. More concept art of her room reveals soccer cleats hinting that she may be a soccer player. It is unknown if these were simply done as character explorations or if they are indicative of Gwen as a character in the films. *The tracks "Self Love" and "Calling" from the ''Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse'' soundtrack both appear to be from the perspective of Gwen with Self Love featuring lyrics pertaining to Gwen leaving her universe in a search for a sense of belong and attempting to repair her friendship with Miles and Calling featuring lyrics pertaining to Gwen's feelings of hurt and guilt over the mistakes she made towards Miles in the film. ** Due to her prolonged absence from her own universe, it is most likely that Gwen will have to repeat her current year of high school. **Gwen, along with Jessica, Margo and the band members of the Mary Janes all share a tooth gap between their two front teeth. * === '''Behind the scenes''': === *Originally, ''Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse'' was set to feature a romance between her and Miles, specifically with Miles and Gwen sharing a kiss before Gwen returns home to her universe. The idea was scrapped so the film could explore both Miles coming into his powers as well as to first introduce Gwen's character for more than her romantic value. The seeds of this romance are explored in ''Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse'' and will be explored more in [[Spider-Man: Beyond the Spider-Verse|''Spider-Man: Beyond the Spider-Verse'']].[https://www.vanityfair.com/hollywood/2018/12/sony-spider-man-future-amy-pascal-phil-lord-interview Sony Finally Untangles Its Spider Web] | Vanity Fair **The shot where Gwen bids her farewell to Miles before heading to Mumbattan mirrors the iconic upside-down kiss scene in Sam Raimi's ''Spider-Man,'' although the two don't kiss here. *Gwen will star in the [[Spider-Women spin-off|''Spider-Women'' spin-off]] alongside [[Jessica Drew (Earth-332)|Jessica Drew]] and Silk. It is currently unknown where the film will take place in the Spider-Verse saga. *In the Portuguese release of ''Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse'' (''Homem-Aranha: No Universo Aranha''), Gwen was voiced by [[wikipedia:Daniela Melchior|Daniela Melchior]].[https://deadline.com/2020/02/the-suicide-squad-james-gunn-movie-actress-daniel-melchior-signs-with-caa-1202862445/ ‘The Suicide Squad’ Star Daniel Melchior Signs With CAA] – Deadline[[w:c:international-dubbing:Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse#European Portuguese|Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse on the International Dubbing wiki]] *In ''Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse'', Gwen is shown to have pink ombre hair, which was a design first seen in [[w:c:marvel:Gwendolyn Poole (Earth-TRN565)|Gwen Poole]] and later adapted to versions of the character in [[wikipedia:Marvel Rising|''Marvel Rising'']] and [[wikipedia:Marvel's Spidey and His Amazing Friends|''Marvel's Spidey and His Amazing Friends'']]. ==References== {{Gallery|Gwendolyn Stacy (Earth-65)/Gallery|Gwendolyn Stacy}} {{WQ|Gwendolyn Stacy (Earth-65)/Quotes|Gwendolyn Stacy}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Stacy, Gwendolyn}} [[Category:Characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man: Into the Spider-Verse characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse characters]] [[Category:Spider-Man: Beyond the Spider-Verse characters]] [[Category:Female characters]] [[Category:Americans]] [[Category:Irishes]] [[Category:Heroes]] [[Category:Humans]] [[Category:Mary Janes Members]] [[Category:Spider-Gang Members]] [[Category:Spider-Society Members]] [[Category:Spider-People]] [[Category:Gwen Stacy]] [[Category:Musicians]] [[Category:Performers]] [[Category:Drummers]] [[Category:Fair Skin]] [[Category:Catholics]] [[Category:Christians]] [[Category:Depression]] [[Category:Post-Traumatic Stress Disorder]] [[Category:Students]] [[Category:Hobie Brown's Band]] [[Category:Earth-65]]
